name: Switch
description: A binary toggle control that lets a user turn a single option on or off. Communicates its current value through thumb position. Supports controlled and uncontrolled usage.

checked.description: The current on/off value of the switch. When provided externally, the component operates in controlled mode.

disabled.description: Prevents all user-initiated interaction. The switch still reflects its current checked value.

defaultChecked.description: Initial value used only in uncontrolled mode. Ignored if checked is provided.

onChange.description: Callback invoked when the user toggles the switch. Receives the new checked value. Not called on programmatic prop changes.

aria-label.description: Accessible name. Required when no visible label is associated with the switch.

aria-labelledby.description: ID of an external element that labels this switch. Alternative to aria-label.

rest.description: Default idle state. No active user interaction.

hovered.description: Pointer is over the component. Indicates interactivity.
entry_conditions: pointerenter fires and disabled is false

focused.description: Component has keyboard focus. Not defined as a Figma variant but required for accessible implementation.
entry_conditions: tab or shift+tab navigates to component

pressed.description: Pointer is held down on the component. Momentary active state.
entry_conditions: pointerdown fires while hovered and not disabled

disabled.description: Interaction is frozen. The component shows its checked value but ignores all user input.

toggle_action.description: Flips the checked value. Can originate from multiple triggers.
toggle_action.effect_controlled: onChange is called with new value; parent is responsible for updating checked prop

enter.effect: transitions to hovered state if not disabled

down.description: Pointer button pressed while over component.
down.effect: transitions to pressed state if not disabled

click.description: Full press-and-release cycle on the component.
click.effect: invokes toggle action if not disabled

tab.effect: moves focus to this component if it is next in tab order

shift_tab.effect: moves focus to this component if it is previous in tab order

space.effect: invokes toggle action when focused and not disabled

enter.effect: invokes toggle action when focused and not disabled

tap.description: Single finger tap on a touch device.
tap.effect: equivalent to pointer.click — invokes toggle action if not disabled

resize.description: Component has a fixed intrinsic size and does not respond to container resize with any behavioral change.

checked_prop_change.description: Parent updates the checked prop value.
checked_prop_change.effect: thumb position updates to reflect new value; onChange is NOT called

disabled_prop_change.description: Parent updates the disabled prop value.
disabled_prop_change.effect: transitions to disabled state (true) or rest state (false)

focus_call.description: Imperative focus() call on the component's DOM node.
focus_call.effect: transitions to focused state if not disabled

toggle.description: The primary interaction — flips the switch between on and off.
toggle.effect: checked value inverts (true → false, false → true)
toggle.mode_controlled: component calls onChange and waits for parent to update checked prop

change.description: Fired whenever the user performs a toggle interaction.

payload.checked: boolean — the new value after the toggle, not the previous value

not_emitted_when: checked prop is changed programmatically by the parent

aria_attributes.aria-checked: mirrors checked value at all times (true | false)
aria_attributes.aria-disabled: mirrors disabled prop when true; omit attribute when false
aria_attributes.aria-label: required when no associated visible label exists
aria_attributes.aria-labelledby: alternative to aria-label — references an external label element

keyboard_navigation.tab_stop: always included in tab order unless disabled

focus_management.focus_visible: a visible focus indicator must appear when focus arrives via keyboard
focus_management.focus_trap: none — focus is not trapped within the component
focus_management.focus_on_disable: if the component holds focus when disabled, focus moves to the next focusable element

screen_reader.on_toggle: the new state ('on' or 'off') must be announced immediately after toggle
screen_reader.on_disable: state change to disabled should be announced if the component is currently focused

responsive.description: The Switch has a fixed intrinsic size. It does not adapt its layout or behavior based on container width or viewport breakpoints. No behavioral differences across sizes.

parent_requirements.description: The Switch is a self-contained leaf component. It places no requirements on its parent beyond receiving the props defined above.
parent_requirements.layout: none — parent can place it anywhere
parent_requirements.context: none — does not consume any React context or provider by default
parent_requirements.label_association: parent is responsible for associating a visible label and passing aria-label or aria-labelledby

mutual_exclusions.rule: hovered and disabled are mutually exclusive states
mutual_exclusions.reason: a disabled switch cannot be interacted with, so a pointer over it must not produce the hovered appearance or behavior

conditional_behaviors.then: all pointer, keyboard, and touch triggers are suppressed; onChange is never called

conditional_behaviors.if: checked prop is provided by parent (controlled mode)
conditional_behaviors.then: the component must not manage its own checked state; it must always reflect the prop and call onChange to request a change

conditional_behaviors.if: defaultChecked is provided without a checked prop (uncontrolled mode)
conditional_behaviors.then: the component owns its checked state from mount onward; defaultChecked is read only once

required_combinations.rule: at least one of aria-label or aria-labelledby must be present or a visible label must be associated
required_combinations.reason: a switch without an accessible name is unusable by screen reader users

scenarios.user_toggles_on: Switch is at rest with checked=false. User clicks or presses Space. onChange fires with checked=true. Thumb animates to the on position.
scenarios.user_toggles_off: Switch is at rest with checked=true. User clicks or presses Space. onChange fires with checked=false. Thumb animates to the off position.
scenarios.keyboard_navigation: User tabs to the switch. The focused state activates. User presses Space. Toggle fires. User tabs away. Focused state exits.
scenarios.disabled_interaction_attempt: Switch has disabled=true. User hovers, clicks, or presses Space. No state change occurs. onChange is not called.
scenarios.controlled_update: Parent receives onChange with checked=true and updates its state. Parent re-renders with checked=true. Switch reflects new value without emitting a second change event.
scenarios.programmatic_disable_while_focused: Switch has keyboard focus. Parent sets disabled=true. Focus moves to the next focusable element. Screen reader announces the change.
